chunk_summary: This section outlines statutory supplementary nutrition norms under NFSA for ICDS, detailing caloric and protein requirements for children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers, alongside operational guidelines for service delivery and clinical troubleshooting.
learning_objectives: Understand the statutory nutrition norms under NFSA for ICDS beneficiaries, including caloric and protein requirements.
Learn how to document entitlement, measured uptake, and delivery mode during service visits.
Identify clinical troubleshooting strategies for malnourished children failing to respond to supplementary nutrition.
Recognize the importance of service continuity and its impact on pediatric growth outcomes.
key_facts: Children 6–35 months: THR 500 kcal and 12–15 g protein/day.
Children 3–6 years: Morning snack + hot cooked meal 500 kcal and 12–15 g protein/day.
Malnourished children 6–59 months: THR 800 kcal and 20–25 g protein/day.
Pregnant women and lactating mothers: 600 kcal and 18–20 g protein/day.
Supplementary Nutrition Rules specify provision for ~300 service days/year.
Missed service days, stockouts, or diluted rations can hinder growth outcomes.
reference_quote: Under Schedule II of the NFSA, audit-ready minimums for ICDS supplementary nutrition are: children 6–35 months receiving THR 500 kcal and 12–15 g protein per day; children 3–6 years receiving morning snack plus hot cooked meal 500 kcal and 12–15 g protein per day; children 6–59 months who are malnourished receiving THR 800 kcal and 20–25 g protein per day; pregnant women and lactating mothers receiving 600 kcal and 18–20 g protein per day.
document_key_topics: Statutory nutrition norms under NFSA for ICDS
Impact of missed service days on pediatric growth
